h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’m sorry, I thought the sign-off sheet was the thing that made it okay to take out there. I did each item, so I’m not sure what else I was supposed to judge before someone else handles the prototype.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think maybe the sheet only says I did those items, not that this is ready for anyone to pick up. I’m worried I was leaning on the clipboard because it felt safer than making the call myself.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to rely on the checklist to justify releasing the prototype.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I’m sorry, I thought the sign-off sheet was the thing that made it okay to take out there. I did each item, so I’m not sure what else I was supposed to judge before someone else handles the prototype.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: even exact wording cannot sit in the exhibit like it explains itself.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to progressively refine how they write captions for the exhibit based on the tutor's guidance.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Let “closed” name access, not motive. If the notes show the committee setting limits, the label can say “may narrow what counts.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's hesitation about accusing the mayor of hiding by redefining 'closed' as access rather than motive.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to probe and challenge the tutor's frameworks for distinguishing legitimate scarcity management from exploitation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the word is too soft. Retire it.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's skepticism about the word 'proportional' by retiring it and introducing a concrete audit standard with specific quantitative thresholds.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to resist the mathematical reality of quadrupled area due to their attachment to the doubling shortcut.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: This is the hidden step. The side doubled in both directions. The area became four times as large, not twice. Say that claim from the grid.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's lingering desire to call the area doubled by physically demonstrating the step-by-step doubling of both dimensions.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
